app.name: QuiverKit
app.tagline: Developer tools that never leave your browser

home.heading: Every tool you keep googling for
home.subheading: Base64, JWT, hashes, UUIDs and more. Everything runs on your machine — no upload, no account, no tracking.

privacy.note: Nothing you type here is sent anywhere.

network.tooltip: This tool has to contact a server. Everything else runs entirely in your browser.

error.emptyInput: Type or paste something to get started.
error.invalidUtf8: Decoded successfully, but the result is binary, not text.
error.invalidJwt: This is not a valid JWT. A token has three dot-separated parts.
error.invalidTimestamp: This is not a date or a Unix timestamp.
error.noCharacterSet: Pick at least one set of characters.
error.invalidColor: This is not a colour we recognise. Try #3b82f6, rgb(59 130 246) or hsl(217 91% 60%).
error.invalidNumber: This number does not belong to the chosen base.
error.invalidPermissions: Use three or four octal digits (755) or nine symbols (rwxr-xr-x).
error.invalidVersion: Use a full version with three parts, like 1.2.3.
error.invalidCidr: Use an address and a prefix, like 192.168.1.0/24.
error.invalidCurl: This does not look like a curl command with a URL.
error.decryptFailed: Could not decrypt. The passphrase is wrong, or the message has been altered.
error.keyGenerationFailed: Your browser cannot generate this kind of key.
error.unsupportedImage: This tool reads JPEG files. Other formats store metadata differently.
error.invalidCron: A cron line has five fields, like 0 9 * * mon-fri.
error.invalidCsv: This is not a table we can read. JSON input must be an array of objects.
error.invalidHex: Hex needs an even number of digits.
error.invalidSvg: This does not contain an <svg> element.
error.invalidBase58: Base58 leaves out 0, O, I and l on purpose.
error.invalidSecret: This is not a valid Base32 secret.
error.invalidDomain: Enter a domain name, like example.com.
error.networkFailed: The request failed. Check your connection, or the name you asked for.
error.noQrFound: No QR code found in this image.
error.invalidCertificate: Could not read this certificate. Try a PEM-formatted X.509 certificate.

base64.name: Base64
base64.description: Encode and decode Base64, with URL-safe output and full Unicode support.

jwt.name: JWT Decoder
jwt.description: Inspect a JSON Web Token's header, payload and expiry — without sending it anywhere.
jwt.secretPlaceholder: HMAC secret, if you have it
jwt.verifyNote: Only the HS256, HS384 and HS512 algorithms can be checked here, because those verify with the same key that signs. Leave the field empty to skip verification.

outcome.unsupported: {{algorithm}} verifies with a public key, which this tool does not carry.

jwt.signatureNote: Nothing you paste here leaves the page, but a production secret pasted into any website should be treated as compromised.

url.name: URL Encoder
url.description: Percent-encode and decode URLs, or split one into its parts.
url.componentHint: Escapes everything unsafe, including / ? & = — use this for a single value.
url.wholeHint: Leaves reserved characters intact — use this for a complete address.
url.parseHint: Splits an address into protocol, host, path and query parameters.

json.name: JSON Formatter
json.description: Format, minify and alphabetically sort JSON.

case.name: Case Converter
case.description: Convert between camelCase, snake_case, kebab-case and more, plus URL slugs.
case.localeNote: Identifier styles ignore the text language on purpose: under Turkish rules "ID" lowercases to "ıd", which no compiler accepts. Prose styles — Title Case, Sentence case, lowercase and UPPERCASE — do follow it, so Turkish turns "istanbul" into "İstanbul" while English gives "Istanbul".

lines.name: Line Sorter
lines.description: Sort lines, remove duplicates and order embedded numbers naturally.
lines.localeNote: Sorting follows the alphabet of the text language, not the interface — in Turkish, ç comes right after c instead of after z.

stats.name: Text Statistics
stats.description: Count characters, words, sentences and reading time in any language.
stats.segmenterNote: Words are counted with Intl.Segmenter, so Japanese and Chinese — written without spaces — are counted correctly rather than as one long word.

timestamp.name: Timestamp Converter
timestamp.description: Convert between Unix time, ISO 8601 and human-readable dates.
timestamp.hint: Accepts Unix seconds, Unix milliseconds or any date string.

hash.name: Hash Generator
hash.description: SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-384 and SHA-512 digests, with optional HMAC.
hash.secretPlaceholder: Leave empty for a plain hash
hash.secretHint: Fill this in to sign the input with HMAC instead of hashing it.
hash.md5Note: MD5 is missing on purpose. Browsers refuse to implement it because it is broken, and adding our own would only encourage its use.

escape.name: Escape & Unescape
escape.description: Escape text for JSON, HTML, SQL, the shell or a regular expression.

hint.json: Produces the inside of a JSON string, without the surrounding quotes.
hint.html: Turns the five characters that break markup into entities.
hint.sql: Doubles the single quote — the form every SQL dialect agrees on. Escaping is not a substitute for parameterised queries.
hint.shell: Wraps the text in single quotes so nothing expands. Safe to paste as one argument.
hint.regex: Escapes the metacharacters so the text matches itself literally.

regex.name: Regex Tester
regex.description: Test a regular expression against text and inspect every match and group.
regex.subject: Test string

diff.name: Text Diff
diff.description: Compare two blocks of text line by line.

invisible.name: Invisible Characters
invisible.description: Find zero-width, look-alike and control characters hiding in text.
invisible.placeholder: Paste the text that will not behave…
invisible.normalise: Turn exotic spaces into ordinary ones
invisible.note: These characters survive a copy-paste unnoticed and are behind a whole genre of bug: a config key that will not match, a password that is rejected, a regex that silently fails.

numberBase.name: Number Base Converter
numberBase.description: Convert between binary, octal, decimal, hexadecimal and any base up to 36.
numberBase.note: Conversion runs on arbitrary-precision integers, so hashes and bit masks past 2^53 stay exact instead of quietly rounding.

dataSize.name: Data Size Converter
dataSize.description: Convert between bytes, kB and KiB, and see where the two disagree.
dataSize.note: A drive maker's 1 TB is 10^12 bytes, while the operating system divides by 1024 and reports 931 GiB. Same drive, different arithmetic.

chmod.name: chmod Calculator
chmod.description: Translate Unix permissions between 755 and rwxr-xr-x.
chmod.note: When a special bit is set on something that cannot be executed, the letter turns capital: 4644 shows as rwSr--r--.

semver.name: Semantic Version
semver.description: Compare versions and check them against a range like ^1.2.0.
semver.note: Supports ^, ~, >, >=, <, <=, =, x wildcards, spaces for AND and || for OR. Hyphen ranges are not covered.

units.name: CSS Units
units.description: Convert between px, rem, em, pt and percent.
units.note: rem is relative to the document, em to the element's parent — which is why nested em values compound. CSS fixes 1in at 96px, so 12pt is exactly 16px on every screen.

gradient.name: Gradient Generator
gradient.description: Build a linear, radial or conic gradient and copy the CSS.

shadow.name: Box Shadow
shadow.description: Design a shadow, stack several layers and copy the CSS.
shadow.note: One hard shadow rarely looks right. Stacking a few layers with growing offset and fading opacity reads as a single soft light source, which is how design systems get their depth.

bezier.name: Cubic Bézier
bezier.description: Shape an easing curve by dragging its handles and preview the motion.
bezier.note: Drag a handle above or below the box to overshoot — that is what gives an animation its bounce. CSS clamps the horizontal axis to 0–1, but not the vertical one.

image.name: Image Converter
image.description: Convert, resize and compress images without uploading them.
image.pending: Press Convert to see the result
image.note: Everything runs through a canvas in your browser. Set max width to 0 to keep the original size. Re-drawing also drops every piece of metadata, since only pixels survive the round trip.

exif.name: EXIF Viewer
exif.description: See what a photo reveals about you, and strip it out.
exif.locationWarning: Anyone who receives this file can read these coordinates. Most social networks strip them; sending the file directly does not.
exif.clean: No metadata found. This file is safe to share as it is.
exif.note: Stripping removes the metadata segments and copies the image data byte for byte, so nothing is re-encoded and no quality is lost. The file never leaves your browser.

baseEncoding.name: Base32 & Base58
baseEncoding.description: The two alphabets Base64 does not cover.

hint.base32: RFC 4648. Uses A–Z and 2–7 only, so it survives being read aloud or typed by hand — which is why authenticator secrets use it.
hint.base58: Bitcoin's alphabet: Base64 without 0, O, I, l and the two symbols, so nothing can be misread. Encoding is a base change on the whole number, not a bit-packing.

punycode.name: Punycode / IDN
punycode.description: Convert an internationalised domain to its xn-- form and back.
punycode.note: Each label is converted on its own, since a dot is never encoded. This is also how homograph attacks hide: two domains can look identical and encode differently.

totp.name: TOTP Generator
totp.description: Produce the six-digit code from a Base32 secret, like an authenticator app.
totp.note: Paste an otpauth:// URI and every field fills itself. The secret never leaves this page — but a secret pasted into any website should be rotated afterwards, and this is a debugging aid rather than a replacement for your authenticator.

vision.name: Colour Vision
vision.description: See how a pair of colours holds up for colour-blind viewers.
vision.note: Deuteranopia alone affects roughly one man in twelve. A palette that separates red from green collapses for them; one that separates blue from yellow survives — which is why status colours should differ in lightness too, not only in hue.

favicon.name: Favicon Generator
favicon.description: Turn one image into every icon size a site needs.
favicon.note: A non-square image is cropped from the centre rather than squashed. Everything is drawn on a canvas here, so the image is never uploaded.

dns.name: DNS Lookup
dns.warning: The lookup is sent to {{resolver}}, which will see the domain you asked about. Every other tool here runs entirely on your machine.
dns.note: Resolving a name means asking a resolver — there is no offline way to do it. The request goes over DNS-over-HTTPS, so your network provider cannot read it, but the resolver can.

cron.name: Cron Expression
cron.description: Read a crontab line and see when it actually runs.
cron.never: This expression never matches a real date.
cron.note: When both the day of month and the day of week are restricted, cron runs on either — not both. "0 0 13 * fri" means every 13th and every Friday, not Friday the 13th.

csv.name: CSV ↔ JSON
csv.description: Convert between CSV and JSON, quoted fields and all.
csv.note: Reading is done character by character rather than by splitting on the delimiter, so a quoted field containing a comma, a newline or a doubled quote survives intact.

jsonTypes.name: JSON to Types
jsonTypes.description: Turn a sample response into TypeScript interfaces or a JSON Schema.
jsonTypes.note: Given an array, a key that appears in only some elements is marked optional — which is what makes the result usable against a real API rather than only against your sample.

timezone.name: Time Zone Converter
timezone.description: See one moment across several time zones at once.
timezone.note: Offsets come from the browser's own time zone database, so daylight saving is handled rather than assumed.

dateDiff.name: Date Difference
dateDiff.description: Count the days, working days and calendar gap between two dates.

reference.name: HTTP Reference
reference.description: Look up status codes and MIME types.

hex.name: Hex Viewer
hex.description: Convert text to hex and back, or dump a file byte by byte.
hex.note: Conversion goes through UTF-8, so text in any script round-trips correctly. The dump shows the first 512 rows.

lorem.name: Lorem Ipsum
lorem.description: The classic Latin placeholder text, by words, sentences or paragraphs.
lorem.note: The same request always produces the same text, so a mock-up does not shuffle itself every time you regenerate it.

cipher.name: Classic Ciphers
cipher.description: ROT13, Caesar, Atbash, reverse and Morse.
cipher.note: These are puzzles, not security — every one of them was broken centuries ago. They are here because CTFs, riddles and school exercises still use them constantly.

sql.name: SQL Formatter
sql.description: Lay a query out on readable lines, or collapse it onto one.
sql.note: Strings and comments are tokenised separately, so a keyword that happens to sit inside a literal is never uppercased — that would change what the query does.

xml.name: XML / HTML Formatter
xml.description: Indent markup, or strip the whitespace back out.
xml.note:
An element whose only child is text stays on one line, and void HTML tags such as
do not open an indent level.

jsonpath.name: JSONPath Query
jsonpath.description: Pull values out of a JSON document with a path expression.
jsonpath.note: Supports $.key, [0], [-1], [*], [1:3] and the recursive ..key. Filter expressions are left out — that is where the grammar turns into a language of its own.

svg.name: SVG Optimiser
svg.description: Strip an SVG down, then export it as a data URI or a JSX component.
svg.note: The data URI percent-encodes only the characters that would break a CSS url(), which keeps it far shorter than base64 — and still readable.

palette.name: Colour Palette
palette.description: Build a tonal scale and matching harmonies from one colour.
palette.note: Built in OKLCH, where equal steps in lightness look equally different to the eye. Chroma is lowered until each step fits inside sRGB — clamping the channels instead would drag the hue and turn a light blue lilac.

mock.name: Mock Data
mock.description: Generate realistic test data as JSON, reproducibly.
mock.note: The same seed always rebuilds the same data, so a fixture stays stable between runs. Names are drawn from several regions rather than one.

cidr.name: Subnet Calculator
cidr.description: Work out the network, broadcast and host range of an IPv4 block.
cidr.note: A /31 carries two addresses and a /32 exactly one, and neither reserves a network or broadcast address the way larger blocks do.

aes.name: AES Encryption
aes.description: Encrypt and decrypt text with a passphrase, entirely in your browser.
aes.note: AES-GCM with a key stretched from your passphrase through 250,000 rounds of PBKDF2. GCM authenticates as well as encrypts, so an altered message refuses to decrypt rather than turning into garbage. The salt and IV travel with the ciphertext — neither is secret.

keypair.name: Key Pair Generator
keypair.description: Generate an RSA, ECDSA or Ed25519 key pair in PEM format.
keypair.note: Keys are generated by your browser's own crypto engine and never leave the page. Still, a key you paste into any website is a key you should treat as compromised — generate production keys with your own tooling.

hint.RSA-2048: The safe default. Widely supported, and fast enough to generate.
hint.RSA-4096: Stronger, noticeably slower to generate and to use.
hint.ECDSA-P256: Much smaller keys at comparable strength. The usual modern choice.
hint.Ed25519: Fast, small and hard to misuse. Needs a recent browser.

checksum.name: File Checksum
checksum.description: Verify a downloaded file against its published hash, without uploading it.
checksum.note: The file is read into memory and hashed on the spot. Nothing is uploaded, which is what makes it reasonable to check a file you would never send to a stranger.

curl.name: cURL to Code
curl.description: Turn a curl command into fetch, axios, Python or Go.
curl.note: Flags that do not change the request — -L, -k, --compressed and friends — are ignored rather than guessed at.

color.name: Colour Converter
color.description: Convert between HEX, RGB, HSL and OKLCH, and check WCAG contrast.
color.sample: Normal text at the size most interfaces use.
color.oklchNote: OKLCH is worth learning: unlike HSL, equal steps in its lightness look equally different to the eye, so palettes built in it stay balanced. Large text means 24px, or 18.66px bold.
color.alphaNote: The WCAG formula only covers opaque colours, so translucent text is flattened onto its background first — fading text really does lower its contrast. A translucent background is flattened onto white, since nothing can be known about the page behind it.

password.name: Password Generator
password.description: Generate strong passwords with unbiased randomness, and see their real strength.
password.entropyNote: Strength is measured in bits of entropy — how many guesses an attacker needs — rather than by whether the password happens to contain a symbol. Every character is drawn with rejection sampling, so no letter is more likely than another.

uuid.name: UUID Generator
uuid.description: Generate UUID v4, time-ordered UUID v7 and Nano IDs.
uuid.v7Hint: Time-ordered, so it sorts by creation. Better as a database key.

markdown.name: Markdown Preview
markdown.description: Write Markdown and see the rendered HTML side by side.

qrcode.name: QR Code
qrcode.description: Generate QR codes from text or decode them from an image.
qrcode.drop: Drop an image of a QR code here

html.name: HTML Stripper
html.description: Strip HTML tags and decode entities back to plain text.

gzip.name: GZip / Brotli
gzip.description: Compress text with GZip or Deflate right in your browser.
gzip.note: Compression uses the browser's built-in CompressionStream API. Nothing is sent anywhere.

cert.name: Certificate Decoder
cert.description: Decode a PEM X.509 certificate and inspect its fields — no upload, all local.
cert.subject: Subject

jsondiff.name: JSON Diff
jsondiff.description: Compare two JSON documents structurally, key by key and value by value.

graphql.name: GraphQL Formatter
graphql.description: Format or minify a GraphQL query, mutation or schema definition.
